1.description: Counties in a hurricane landfall region. This figure shows the counties that are within 50, 100, 150, and 200 miles of the eye of the hurricane at landfall for four selected hurricanes from our sample of 37 hurricane landfalls.

2.description:
Hurricane forecasts by day and wind speed probability threshold. This figure presents an example of the processed wind speed forecast data. Each map shows the counties included in the forecast path for Hurricane Sandy given the number of days before landfall in each row (from four days to one day before landfall) and the wind speed probability threshold in each column (

3.description: Firm establishments by county. This figure depicts counties based on the number of establishments located in that county in 2010. Counties are sorted into deciles based on the number of establishments for the firms in our sample. The darker the shade, the greater the number of establishments in a county.

5.description: Uncertainty after hurricane landfall. Panel A plots coefficient estimates from running separate regressions estimating specification (7) when varying the number of trading days after landfall. Changes in implied volatilities from the day before hurricane inception to up to 120 trading days (six months) post-hurricane landfall are regressed on the share of firm establishments in the landfall region. A coefficient estimate of, for example, 5 means that a firm with all of its establishments in the landfall region is estimated to experience a 5% increase in implied volatility, relative to control firms with no establishments in the landfall region. The landfall region is based on a 200-mile radius around the hurricane eye. Confidence bands of 95% are shown. Panel B depicts the timeline of discussions of hurricanes in Refinitiv transcripts of calls between analysts, investors, and firm management for hurricane-hit and control firms. Each point reflects the average number of paragraphs per call discussing hurricanes over the given week after landfall. For firms with positive exposure to the landfall region (hit firms), the number of paragraphs are weighted by the firm's landfall exposure. A value of, for example, 3 means that a firm with all of its establishments in the landfall region has three paragraphs per call discussing hurricanes.

6.description: Changes in volatility risk premium after hurricane landfall. This figure plots coefficient estimates when regressing VRP averaged over increments of five trading days on the landfall region exposure of a firm, as shown in equation (8). VRP is the difference between ex ante implied and ex post realized volatility, as shown in equation (2). A coefficient estimate of, for example, −5 means that a firm with all of its establishments in the landfall region is estimated to have a 5 percentage point lower VRP than control firms. Panel A shows estimates for the subsample of hurricanes from 1996 up to and including Hurricane Sandy in 2012. Panel B shows estimates for the subsample of post-Sandy hurricanes from 2013 to 2019. The landfall region is based on a 200-mile radius around the eye of the hurricane. Confidence bands of 95% are shown.
